entries.title: Visible safeguard / refusal message
entries.user_sees: A message declining or redirecting the request, often mentioning that it cannot help with the request as phrased.
entries.api_correlate: On the API this class of outcome corresponds to a response with stop_reason 'refusal', optionally with a stop_details category.
entries.can_conclude: That the request, as submitted, produced a decline the user could see. The visible text is the evidence.
entries.cannot_conclude: Why it was declined, which specific classifier or rule applied, or any threshold. Do not infer internal moderation logic.

entries.title: Visible model switch after a safeguard event
entries.user_sees: An indication that the response was produced by a different model than requested (e.g., a request intended for one model answered by Claude Opus 4.8), sometimes alongside or after a safeguard notice.
entries.api_correlate: On the API, a configured fallback surfaces as a content block of type 'fallback' (from/to models) and the response 'model' field names the serving model. Whether a given consumer surface uses this mechanism or displays a switch notice is treated as user-reported observation until clm-consumer-surface-fallback is verified.
entries.can_conclude: That the user observed a switch in which model answered. The switch the user saw is a fact about their screen.
entries.cannot_conclude: The mechanism a specific consumer surface used, or that a switch always follows a decline. Cite only the documented API mechanism; attribute the surface behavior to the user's report.

entries.title: API stop_reason: refusal
entries.user_sees: A JSON response with stop_reason 'refusal' and possibly a stop_details object.
entries.api_correlate: stop_reason 'refusal'; stop_details.category among documented values or null.
entries.can_conclude: That the API declined this request and, if present, the documented category label.
entries.cannot_conclude: The classifier internals behind the category, or thresholds. category may be null even on a refusal.

entries.title: API fallback content block
entries.user_sees: A content block of type 'fallback' with from/to model fields; the response 'model' names the serving model.
entries.can_conclude: That a configured fallback served the response and which model did.
entries.cannot_conclude: That fallback implies a policy decline in every case, or any internal routing detail.

entries.title: User-visible error
entries.user_sees: An error such as rate limit, request too large, overloaded, or a max_tokens / context-window stop.
entries.api_correlate: Documented HTTP error codes and stop reasons (max_tokens, model_context_window_exceeded).
entries.can_conclude: The documented meaning of the specific error code or stop reason the user saw.
entries.cannot_conclude: Provider-side capacity state or anything beyond the documented meaning.

entries.title: No observable event
entries.user_sees: Nothing unusual; a pure prompt-quality review with no event reported.
entries.can_conclude: That the user is asking for prompt-quality help, not event explanation.
